metadata.changes:
Change 1: Replace:
  if p == "manager":
              base = f"{s} is managed by {o}"
with:
  if p == "manager":
              # Use a more natural phrasing: object is the manager of subject
              base = f"{o} is the manager of {s}"
Change 2: Replace:
  if p == "draftRound":
              base = f"{s} was selected in round {o}"
with:
  if p == "draftRound":
              # Remove surrounding quotes if present and use natural phrasing
              o_clean = o.strip('"').strip("'")
              base = f"{s}'s draft round was {o_clean}"
Change 3: Replace:
  if p == "youthclub":
              base = f"{s} youthclub {o}"
with:
  if p == "youthclub":
              # Natural phrasing for youth club information
              o_clean = o.strip('"').strip("'")
              base = f"{s}'s youth club was {o_clean}"
Change 4: '# clubs = groups.get("club", []) + groups.get("currentclub", [])' to '# clubs = groups.get("club", []) + groups.get("currentclub", [])'
Change 5: Replace:
  if "manager" in groups:
              parts.append(f"{s} is managed by {join_items(groups['manager'])}")
with:
  # Removed explicit manager handling to allow render() to produce
          # more natural phrasing (e.g., "Valery Petrakov is the manager of FC Torpedo Moscow").
Change 6: Replace:
  handled = {
              "birthDate", "birthYear", "birthPlace", "height", "weight",
              "position", "club", "currentclub", "formerTeam", "debutTeam",
              "league", "manager", "coach"
          }
with:
  handled = {
              "birthDate", "birthYear", "birthPlace", "height", "weight",
              "position", "club", "currentclub", "formerTeam", "debutTeam",
              "league", "coach"
          }
